title: Apply family semantics
description: lapply, sapply, vapply, and purrr::map differ in return-type guarantees and simplification.
body: Use when applying a function over a collection in R. lapply always returns a list; sapply tries to simplify but can return a list, vector, or matrix depending on inputs, making its type unstable; vapply requires a declared return template and fails fast on mismatch; purrr::map has typed variants (map_dbl, map_chr). Do not use sapply where the return type must be stable. Actions: prefer vapply or a typed map for predictable output. Validation signals include the returned object having the declared type. Risk boundary: sapply's silent simplification can change a downstream contract without warning.
